post_rendered:
Mafia Game 321
The Mafiahiker's Game to the Galaxy
  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • Day chat will be enabled via an AoPS private forum.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • Do not imitate the mods.
    • There is no confirm rule in this game.
  • Voting/Elimination
    • This game will partially use the voting rules philosophy of "if it looks like a vote, it's a vote". So spelling/capitalization does not matter so long as the “Vote:” part is right, and the following two rules are met.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>", or c or code text: 
      Vote: <insert player here>
      .
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • You may vote for no elimination to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Elimination" or: 
      Vote: No Elimination
      .
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though you have the ability to do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote" or: 
      Unvote
      . An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • If the deadline is met the player with the most votes is eliminated. If there is a tie, the player who received the votes first is eliminated. If No Elimination is part of the tie, No Elimination occurs.
    • Starting day 2, If more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be eliminated. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • Starting day 2, If at least half of the votes are for no elimination, then there will be no elimination. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, it will be twilight and no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post. This game does not use hybrid elimination.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I (or the comod) post a end-of-day scene, the person eliminated (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Each day will be 288 hours (12 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 288 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 48 hours after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • If a player does not make 2 game-advancing posts in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player receives two consecutive PM prods or accumulates a total of 4 PM prods, they will be replaced, unless they make a number of posts deemed sufficient by the moderator to avoid replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, they must PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language or make personal attacks.
    • Be mature.
    • The game is to not be considered over until the mod declares it as such.
    • Don't intentionally/purposely gamethrow. If you believe you can no longer win talk to me in your role pm.
    • For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, they may be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators
    • I (scannose) am the moderator of Mafia Game 321.
    • This turquoise-ish color (#76a5af) is my mod color. Do not post in anything too similar.
    • The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will be based on The Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy book series written by Douglas Adams. Only characters from the first book will be featured.

This game will imitate fdas style setups, which means it may contain the following features:
- only the alignment and flavorname of a player will be revealed upon death, but not their role pm
- this game may include third party roles
- this game may include BM or experimental mechanics
- flavorclaiming and role claiming are allowed unless stated otherwise
- public mod flavor, such as sod or eod flavor, will not be alignment indicative.

vt role pm
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 321.

You are a Dolphin, an individual of an intelligent species that escaped Earth before its destruction. Unfortunately, you have no abilities other than your voice than your vote.

You win when all threats to the Galaxy are eliminated. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Anyway, PeachFox please read this when you have time (or over night): https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Observer

TLDR; You would want to look towards targeting a "popular" player, one that may attract multiple actions.
You are most useful in a massclaim, when you could be used to verify or expose claims.

For example, if player X doctors me tonight and player Y NKs me, and you target me, you would know that I was doctored and NKed. In massclaim (when all of our PRs claim to try and find the mafia through POE), if we have 2 doctor claims (but only one claims to doctor me during N1), you would know which was real and which was false.

post_rendered:
I'm clarifying that my stance on Avy and Violet is still neutral, as I have not found any serious tell that would lend to either alignment. That said, I think it's important for people to be aware that Tintin's argument against Violet and Sweetbird (being opportunistic) is weaker than he made it out to be, since
a) It is very early in the game;
b) These votes are obviously non-serious, and the intention of starting a long-term wagon seems weak or nonexistent;
c) Violet and Sweetbird have tended to lolcat, cast non-serious votes, and make sarcastic arguments in past games.

I don't think pointing out Tintin's exaggerated point to thread should be considered scummy. It's a valid point with an easily traceable line of thought, not some obscure "defense" of Violet or Sweetbird.

Simply put, I believe Tintin misrepresented Violet and Sweetbird's intentions, and I called them out on that. I think the Avy/Tintin interaction is V/W, but my stance isn't particularly strong. I don't have any strong opinions on Violet either.

post_rendered:
Wow, strife, glad to see you solving!!

To answer your question about why I get 'annoyed' or 'push back hard' when voted, early D1 is completely about creating reads. When people vote for me without giving reasons (or when they have bad logic), I push back hard to see how they react. It helps me figure out who has real arguments and who is just following a trend to stay out of suspicion. If you look at my reads, that's how I originally started suspecting SB and BP.

You asked why I've been so against avy. I don't like the way Avy has been playing this game. Avy gets called out for bad logic and immediately drops a 'im feeling lazy give me a few days to get back into it'. Excuse me if this sounds scummy. Also, his original push on me was based off of bad logic and a misinterpretation, which is why I've been especially wary of him throughout this game.

post_rendered: British Petroleum makes the most delicious gasoline! I've tried all the others, and believe me, none of them come close. Chevron's gasoline tastes disgusting. Shell and Exxon are a little bit better, but you honestly have to get premium unleaded to be able to get the flavor notes that you and me both crave. British Petroleum truly has something for everyone, though. For an aromatic and rich tasting gasoline, you can't go wrong with any of their unleaded selections. If you're looking for something sweeter, the diesel (from the green nozzle) is always available as an alternative. Remember, gas pumps are set up to dispense gas until the tank is full. Since you'd burst if you filled up with gas, you'd have to do something else. Walk into the convenience store, where the attendant sits. Pre-pay for a certain amount of gas - I'd suggest starting off with a quarter of a gallon. Then, walk back to the pump, pull the handle on the gas pump, and enjoy! If you have any questions or concerns, feel free to let me know, and I'll do my best to share with you useful info/tips that I've accumulated over time.
post_canonical: British Petroleum makes the most delicious gasoline! I've tried all the others, and believe me, none of them come close. Chevron's gasoline tastes disgusting. [img width=7]https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/unpaidinterncentral/uic-aops-mafia-userscript@42dfd8c2a09447ca41f1096db5adae34fe936a28/assets/emojis/sob.png[/img] Shell and Exxon are a little bit better, but you honestly have to get premium unleaded to be able to get the flavor notes that you and me both crave. British Petroleum truly has something for everyone, though. For an aromatic and rich tasting gasoline, you can't go wrong with any of their unleaded selections. If you're looking for something sweeter, the diesel (from the green nozzle) is always available as an alternative. Remember, gas pumps are set up to dispense gas until the tank is full. Since you'd burst if you filled up with gas, you'd have to do something else. Walk into the convenience store, where the attendant sits. Pre-pay for a certain amount of gas - I'd suggest starting off with a quarter of a gallon. Then, walk back to the pump, pull the handle on the gas pump, and enjoy! If you have any questions or concerns, feel free to let me know, and I'll do my best to share with you useful info/tips that I've accumulated over time.

post_rendered:
tintin21 in p#1580 wrote:
Twin, please don't drink gasoline, it's flammable and thus very dangerous for Triple T's like ourselves.

Don't worry. I only have it in moderation, which is the way to go! It's honestly best to think of gasoline as a special treat that you only get to have some times, like dessert. However, if you're still concerned, my suggestion is to put the gasoline in a can instead of drinking it right at the pump. Then, using a hose, or a sink, you can dilute the gasoline, making it much safer (and even healthier) to consume.

but my goal is to disprove this. People have expressed concerns about my read quality and their strength.
Especially after MG 320 has completed, I would like to give some of my insights into how I go about solving the game. I never really find myself "sheeping" other people's reads, mostly because I can't trust them as town. I like to get a read (even a weak one is fine) on as many slots as I can.
Day 1, for me, is gaining familiarity with all of the slots in the game and how I feel they are playing. I tend to scumlean/scumread a lot of players in this phase since my goal is to establish a town core that I am confident in. I did this successfully in MG 320, having tintin, sweetbird, and BP as 3 of these slots. The rest of the days are for refining the POE and finding the mafia.

I would say that I am a cynical mafia player. Generally if I had to label a player as being either town or mafia (assuming that they are NAI), I naturally lean towards them being mafia. I severely dislike misclearing slots (and this is part of the reason why I have lost in some of my town games), and I feel that it is better to err on the side of caution. IMO, putting a player under pressure is a good way to read their alignment. Townies may struggle sometimes and may freeze up, but I am able to tell (for the most part) whether their reactions are towny or wolfy. It also helps them improve as players, which is also one of my goals.

Now let's talk about my reads in this game.
I opened the game with a wolfread on Azure. I read into "fluff" that everyone else disregarded, and I disliked their attempt to try and pocket BP. MG 320 also showed a similar pattern, with Azure successfully pocketing BP: p#1466 by NoSignOfTheta

Here are all of his posts on Azure. I think that his position at p#798 is particularly telling. He ends up believing that I do not have much on Azure (which is true, there was not much to go off of), and from that, concluded that {me, Azure} is likely {w, v}. He doesn't explicitly say that he believes that Azure is a townie, but he says that his gut tells him that I am a wolf (because of my Azure push), so this can be inferenced.
I honestly think that p#849 is a slip from them and is TMI that Azure is town. But that's not a mature argument so I'll leave that alone for now, would be notable if avyaank does flip W.

Only 40 posts after this stance (where he had Azure at rand!towny and me at rand!wolfy), he posts p#888 which basically creates a separation in the game from SOD to the current state. He says that he would vote Azure if he continues to fluff (because the choice of fluffing vs contribute and choosing to fluff is wolfy) which is somewhat reasonable on surface level. But it's very 1-dimensional and he never really considers what Azure would do as wolf vs town. Actually, they say that if Azure is a wolf, they would coast on the towncred that the received (from CW and violet defending them)
The first part of p#888 ("If azure doesnt make a game-advancing post by the end of today i will vote him") also reads as wolfy to me because these "conditional" votes are much more convenient and easy for wolves to pull off. It shows a lack of consideration for Azure's alignment again, and I think that everyone had a pretty good idea that Azure was going to continue gimmicking (after switching from morse code to emojis before this post).

TLDR; Avyaank read me as wolfy for my push on Azure, and in this process, read Azure as towny (by acknowledging the "towncred" that they gained). Immediately after this, they were suddenly okay with considering Azure as the hang (if they continued fluffing, which was likely).

Here are all of his posts on Azure. I think that his position at p#798 is particularly telling. He ends up believing that I do not have much on Azure (which is true, there was not much to go off of), and from that, concluded that {me, Azure} is likely {w, v}. He doesn't explicitly say that he believes that Azure is a townie, but he says that his gut tells him that I am a wolf (because of my Azure push), so this can be inferenced.
I honestly think that p#849 is a slip from them and is TMI that Azure is town. But that's not a mature argument so I'll leave that alone for now, would be notable if avyaank does flip W.

Only 40 posts after this stance (where he had Azure at rand!towny and me at rand!wolfy), he posts p#888 which basically creates a separation in the game from SOD to the current state. He says that he would vote Azure if he continues to fluff (because the choice of fluffing vs contribute and choosing to fluff is wolfy) which is somewhat reasonable on surface level. But it's very 1-dimensional and he never really considers what Azure would do as wolf vs town. Actually, they say that if Azure is a wolf, they would coast on the towncred that the received (from CW and violet defending them)
The first part of p#888 ("If azure doesnt make a game-advancing post by the end of today i will vote him") also reads as wolfy to me because these "conditional" votes are much more convenient and easy for wolves to pull off. It shows a lack of consideration for Azure's alignment again, and I think that everyone had a pretty good idea that Azure was going to continue gimmicking (after switching from morse code to emojis before this post).

TLDR; Avyaank read me as wolfy for my push on Azure, and in this process, read Azure as towny (by acknowledging the "towncred" that they gained). Immediately after this, they were suddenly okay with considering Azure as the hang (if they continued fluffing, which was likely).

the issue imo is that i think scum tries to make a strong read on azure. if azure is town, its an easy way to farm towncred for later on in the game (who wouldn't take that) and if azure is scum i think scum would usually be nervous about the appearance of fencesitting (i.e. giving a light defense of azure probably looks less suspicious later-game then overtly fencesitting). this is subject to wifom but i feel like the way avy has approached the azure read is more in line with a low-info perspective
im also going to add that avyaank was sort of inactive and i think its 100% possible someone comes into the thread, sees someone pushing for what is essentially a PL, says "erm no PLs are bad", and then the PL target keeps spamming and avyaank says fine i might be willing to pl.

i do agree that conditional votes are bad because it amounts to trying to make a paper trail but at the end of the day its just saying "if u spam ill PL u and if u dont spam i wont" which is the literal purpose of a PL.

first, I'll explain my azure thought process. now even though I distinctly remember theta making a point against azure for his morse code spam, I probably misunderstood. that's myf. the reason I didn't like thetas "push" against azure was because I felt sod1 fluff wasn't ai. obviously, nothing much happened right in the beginning and fluff is pretty normal there, so you can understand where I'm coming from.
theta actually found an argument against me that he can use to his advantage. what I'm talking about is the fact that he thinks that my gut read on him was influenced by his azure read. it wasn't, is all I can say and I'm sure it makes sense that I can't elaborate further. the timestamps sort of prove his point and I can't say anything except "trust me bro I didn't,". so, me sticking on that is pointless. I'll just say that I read thetas argument against azure after I posted that. now I'll explain why i put the conditional vote. first off, fmpov v!azure would probably stop doing this after a while and start posting. it's hard to read azure from this alone because imo there's nothing that is ai in his fluff. even still, I don't expect v!azure to start coasting and doing nothing helpful the entire game, which is mainly why I placed that condition because if he does, then tbh ill find that wolfy and I don't know why everybody was/is seemingly fine with that. as for that towncred post, i def could've worded that better. my point was that except for theta nobody was really scumreading/pushing him (I'm pretty sure this is true), so if he continued to fluff, then eventually someone would start to notice, but not for a while.
thetas point is that apparently, I sr him because of his push, and I tr azure, and now I'm flipping. now if theta is wolf, this would be a good argument that I can't do much against. for one I'll say again that I did not sr him, I just didn't really like that post, and I also didn't townread azure. if you think that when I said "what's the case against him except sod fluff" I was just asking, because I didn't think that was ai (like I said a lot of times)
theta is also saying that my reads aren't too strong and that gives me a tentative state or something, but I would like to argue that a lot of players don't have strong reads, and ill also admit I've been p inactive and haven't gotten the chance to analyze each slot.

I think that's all against me, lmk if you want me to respond to anything more.

fdas wrote:
You are Focalors, a member of the Green Team. You are the Hydro Archon, the goddess of Justice. With the power of water at your command, your abilities when you choose to visit someone are vast. You can watch their surroundings, seeing who visited them that night, or you may confine them to a bubble of water, both preventing them from taking any actions and preventing others from targeting them, or you can declare a judgement upon them, nightkilling them if an investigative action would show them to be a member of the Red Team, Brown Team, or Purple Team. You are also 3 shot bulletproof.

However, despite all the power at your disposal, the tyranny of the Heavenly Principles, and the prophecy they impose, is inexorable. In order to provide salvation for the people, you must give up your life and allow yourself to be executed. To this end, you have an accomplice in this scheme, your other half which will bear the role of Furina on this stage. They have agreed to bear the burden of putting on an unending act to fool the Heavenly Principles.

To that end, they will pretend to be you and can reveal the truth to no one. If they die before you are hanged, their role will be revealed as Focalors and you will die as well, without your role being revealed, at the end of night, without being able to carry out any actions. Their role will also be treated as though it was Focalors for any investigative action, excluding your judgement. Once you are hanged, these effects will no longer apply to them. If you die without being hanged, your role will not be revealed and these effects will remain in place for the rest of the game. You are immune to investigative actions.

You may not make any statements revealing, or hinting at your role name, flavor, win condition, or any other non public information about your role or Furina's role, except for making claims about your night actions and results.

You win if you are hanged and at the end of the game, all members of the Red Team, Brown Team, and Purple Team are dead.
